campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell's city council met to discuss updating the business license tax rates that companies pay to operate in the city. Shannon Dragman from the finance department presented the proposed changes to these tax rates, and the council discussed the matter before deciding whether to approve the new ordinance.
keyTopics: Business license tax rate increases
Annual tax rate update for fiscal year
Finance department presentation on new rates
City council deliberation and decision on ordinance

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council moved forward by introducing an ordinance (a new city law) and skipped the usual first reading process to speed things up. The council then scheduled the ordinance to be voted on and approved at the next regular meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked (first reading waived)
Ordinance scheduled for final approval at next council meeting
Specific ordinance topic not detailed in agenda summary

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026, to approve previous meeting notes, review the city's finances from February, and authorize the Town Manager to sign several construction and professional service contracts. The council discussed various improvement projects and agreements that will move forward with management's approval.
keyTopics: Approval of construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other service providers
February 2026 financial and investment report review
Town improvements resolution and planning authorization
Three-year professional services agreement
Formal approval of previous April 7 meeting minutes

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 28, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve several items including the use of the Janet Gray Hayes Rotunda for a community luncheon event, new pay and contract terms for park rangers and peace officers, and agreements related to security initiatives in the Bay Area. The council also discussed rules about affordable housing restrictions for city properties.
keyTopics: Free use of City Hall Rotunda approved for 'Together San José' community luncheon
New contract and pay adjustments for park rangers and peace officers
Bay Area security initiative agreements and funding
Affordable housing rules and protections for city-owned properties

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 28, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to review a draft plan for improving the city's trees and wildlife habitats, and to celebrate Rengstorff Park's aquatics center receiving a state award for excellence. The council also recognized Fair Housing Month during the meeting.
keyTopics: Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan - draft review and feedback
Rengstorff Park Aquatics Center wins California Parks and Recreation Society Award
Fair Housing Month proclamation
Plans for improving trees and green spaces throughout Mountain View

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special evening meeting on April 28, 2026 to interview candidates for open positions on various city boards and commissions. Residents were able to participate both in person and online to watch the interviews and provide public comment.
keyTopics: Board and commission candidate interviews
Public comment opportunity for residents
In-person and online participation options
Special study session format

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held an Audit Committee meeting where residents were invited to speak about any city matters for up to 3 minutes each. The committee approved meeting minutes from January and reviewed how the city's retirement and health benefit funds performed in the first quarter of 2026.
keyTopics: Public comment period on city issues
Approval of January 26 meeting minutes
Retirement and health benefit fund performance (Q1 2026)
Restrictions on making decisions about topics not on the agenda

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large decorative signs on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup events, and to approve a new labor agreement with the city's firefighters for 2026-2029. One item was withdrawn by staff before the meeting.
keyTopics: Temporary supergraphic signs permitted near Levi's Stadium during World Cup 2026
New three-year contract agreement with Santa Clara firefighters (Local 1171)
Staff withdrew one agenda item before the meeting

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 21, 2026 to make decisions on taxes, development fees, and homelessness services. They held public hearings on reissuing tax rules, approving next year's development budget, and updating various fees that residents and businesses pay to the city.
keyTopics: Tax Equity and Fiscal Responsibility Act reissuance
FY 2026-27 development budget and fees
Updates to user fees and service charges
Santa Clara County homelessness prevention plan
City Council meeting calendar and minutes

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The city council reviewed a request to take another look at a tree removal permit that was already approved for a property at 751 Waverley Street. Someone asked a city planner to reconsider the decision to allow protected trees to be cut down on this property.
keyTopics: 751 Waverley Street tree removal permit under review
Request to reconsider an already-approved permit decision
Protected trees at the center of the dispute
Fast-track planning process (over-the-counter entitlement)
